title: The Case of the Missing Honey
synopsis: When the honey disappears from the savanna, young detective Nia must follow clues and interview animal friends to solve the mystery and discover who took the sweet treat.

pages.text: Nia loved solving mysteries. She lived near the big savanna where many animals were her friends. One morning, Mama Bee buzzed to Nia with tears in her eyes. "Help me, Nia!" she cried. "Someone took all our honey from the hive!" Nia put on her detective hat. "Don't worry, Mama Bee. I will find your honey!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes stands next to a large baobab tree wearing a small detective hat, speaking with a worried-looking bee hovering nearby, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors
illustration.alt: Nia wearing a detective hat talking to a worried bee by a baobab tree

pages.text: Nia looked at the empty hive. She saw sticky paw prints on the tree trunk. "These are clues!" she said. The paw prints were big and round. They led away from the hive toward the tall golden grass. Nia followed the trail carefully. She was a very good detective.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes crouching down to examine large paw prints on a tree trunk, surrounded by golden grass and orange sunset light, painted with bold confident strokes in gouache
illustration.alt: Nia examining paw prints on a tree trunk

pages.text: The paw prints led to a watering hole. Elephant was drinking water. His trunk was sticky! "Hello, Elephant," said Nia. "Did you see who took the honey?" Elephant looked guilty. "I smelled something sweet this morning," he said. "But I didn't take it. I only eat plants!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes talking to a large grey elephant with a sticky trunk at a blue watering hole, painted in textured painterly gouache style with expressive animals
illustration.alt: Nia talking to an elephant with a sticky trunk at a watering hole

pages.text: Nia believed Elephant. She kept following the paw prints. They led to a big rock where Lion was taking a nap. His mane had sticky spots! "Excuse me, Lion," said Nia politely. "Do you know about the missing honey?" Lion yawned. "I was sleeping all morning," he said. "But I heard someone running around early."

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing near a sleepy lion with sticky spots in his mane, lying on a large rock under warm savanna light, painted with thick brushstrokes in gouache
illustration.alt: Nia talking to a sleepy lion with sticky mane spots on a rock

pages.text: The trail continued to a shady spot under an acacia tree. Monkey was swinging from branch to branch. His paws were very sticky! "Hi Monkey!" called Nia. "Your paws look sticky. Did you take Mama Bee's honey?" Monkey shook his head. "I love sweet fruit, but I didn't take any honey. I was picking mangoes all morning!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes looking up at a brown monkey swinging from acacia tree branches with sticky paws, painted in warm savanna colors with textured painterly brushstrokes
illustration.alt: Nia looking up at a monkey with sticky paws in an acacia tree

pages.text: Nia was puzzled. Many animals had sticky paws, but none of them took the honey. She sat down to think. Then she heard a small sound. "Slurp, slurp, slurp." It was coming from behind a big termite hill. Nia tiptoed quietly toward the sound. What could it be?

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes sitting thoughtfully in golden grass near a large termite hill, with orange sunset colors and bold confident gouache strokes creating a mysterious atmosphere
illustration.alt: Nia sitting thoughtfully in the grass near a termite hill

pages.text: Behind the termite hill, Nia found little Bear Cub! He was covered in honey from head to toe. The missing honey pot was right next to him, almost empty. "Oh no!" said Bear Cub. "I'm sorry! I was so hungry and the honey smelled so good. I didn't mean to take it all!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes discovering a small brown bear cub covered in golden honey sitting next to an empty honey pot, painted with expressive animals and thick brushstrokes in gouache
illustration.alt: Nia finding a honey-covered bear cub with an empty honey pot

pages.text: Nia smiled kindly at Bear Cub. "It's okay, but you need to say sorry to Mama Bee." They walked back to the hive together. Bear Cub apologized to Mama Bee. "I'll help you make more honey," he promised. "I can bring you the best flowers!" Mama Bee forgave him, and Nia solved another mystery! Everyone was happy again.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing with a clean bear cub and Mama Bee near the hive, all looking happy as orange sunset light bathes the scene, painted in warm savanna colors with bold confident strokes
illustration.alt: Nia, bear cub, and Mama Bee standing happily together by the hive

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was missing from Mama Bee's hive?
questions.explanation: Mama Bee told Nia that someone took all the honey from her hive.

questions.question: What clues did Nia find at the hive?
questions.explanation: Nia saw sticky paw prints on the tree trunk that led away from the hive.

questions.question: Which animals had sticky spots on them?
questions.explanation: Elephant's trunk was sticky, Lion had sticky spots in his mane, and Monkey had sticky paws.

questions.question: Who really took the honey?
questions.explanation: Nia found Bear Cub behind the termite hill covered in honey with the empty honey pot.

questions.question: How did Bear Cub make up for taking the honey?
choices: promised to bring the best flowers
questions.explanation: Bear Cub promised to help make more honey by bringing Mama Bee the best flowers.
